commit_msg: fix(pipeline): handle missing event loop in fetch_actions
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Loop That Wouldn't Close
subtitle: The event loop was missing. The pipeline was broken. The alibi was falling apart.
when_where: 🌆 2:18 PM on main, the moment the system started to crack. The fetch_actions pipeline had gone silent—no event loop to drive it, no heartbeat, just dead air. 🔦 Omkar saw the error logs light up like a confession written in red. The cover was slipping.
who_whom: 🚨 The pipeline was supposed to be invisible, autonomous, running in the background like a perfect crime. But something had broken—the missing event loop meant the entire operation was exposed, stalled, waiting. 🧩 Omkar had to fix it *now*, before anyone noticed the gap, before the logs told the whole story.
what_why: 🔧 He didn't fix the pipeline because it was broken; he fixed it because it was *visible* while broken. The missing event loop had turned the invisible machine into a corpse on the table, and corpses ask questions. 💀 Every line of the fix was damage control—handle the missing loop, smooth over the error, make it work again so it could go back to working in the dark. 🧠 But he knew now that systems fail. That the crime has cracks. That eventually, the loop always breaks.
verdict: He didn't repair the pipeline; he buried the evidence before the investigation could begin.
